product: SAP S/4HANA — Gateway OData V2 write channel (Business Partner A2X $batch)

csrf.fetch: pre-flight HEAD (GET also legal) to the service root with header x-csrf-token: fetch; the gateway answers with the token in the x-csrf-token response header AND session cookies in Set-Cookie headers
csrf.use: every modifying request carries the fetched x-csrf-token header plus the session cookies (name=value of each Set-Cookie, joined with '; '); token and cookies are cached — one pre-flight serves consecutive batches
csrf.rejection: a stale/invalid token is answered 403 with response header x-csrf-token: Required and body 'CSRF token validation failed'; the client refreshes the token once and retries the SAME batch once; a second consecutive rejection surfaces as an error that never contains token values

batch_request.endpoint: POST <service root>/$batch with Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=<batch boundary>
batch_request.part_headers: every part: Content-Type: application/http and Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary; CRLF line endings throughout
batch_request.retrieve_parts: GET operations are individual top-level parts (a change set cannot contain retrieve requests); embedded request line uses a service-root-relative URI: GET A_BusinessPartner('id')?$format=json HTTP/1.1 with Accept: application/json
batch_request.changesets: writes are grouped in a nested multipart/mixed part with its own changeset boundary; each operation part additionally carries Content-ID: <n> (unique within the change set); embedded requests: POST <entity set> HTTP/1.1 / MERGE <entity>(...) HTTP/1.1 with Content-Type: application/json and the raw JSON body
batch_request.termination: change set closes with --<changeset boundary>--, the batch with --<batch boundary>--, each on its own CRLF-terminated line
batch_request.part_order: parts serialize in the order they were added

batch_response.status: 202 Accepted with Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=<response boundary>
batch_response.structure: one response part per retrieve request and per change set, in request order; each application/http part embeds a full HTTP response (status line, headers such as Content-ID and Location, blank line, body)
batch_response.per_operation: created entities answer 201 with a d-enveloped body and Location; MERGE/PUT answer 204 with no body; results correlate to requests by Content-ID
batch_response.atomic_failure: if any operation of a change set fails, the gateway applies NONE of them and the change-set part contains a single application/http error response (no Content-ID) instead of per-operation responses; the client must report the whole change set as failed with zero applied operations
batch_response.error_document: {"error": {"code", "message": {"lang", "value"}, "innererror": {application.component_id, transactionid, ...}}} — code and message.value are decoded, the raw error JSON is preserved verbatim

mock_fixture.boundaries: injected via the builder constructor (batch_fx01 / changeset_fx01) so the payload is deterministic
